seoTitle: How to Set a Lunch Status in Slack (2026)
metaDescription: Set an out-to-lunch status in Slack with an auto-expiring timer and paused notifications so your team knows when you're away.
intro: Letting your team know you're stepping away for a meal helps set clear expectations and cuts down on notification noise. This guide covers setting the lunch status preset, customizing it, and having it clear automatically.

steps.heading: Open your status menu
steps.body: Click your profile avatar in the lower left corner of the Slack desktop app.

steps.heading: Click Update Your Status
steps.body: Select the status input box to open the configuration window.

steps.heading: Choose the lunch preset
steps.body: Pick 'Out to Lunch' from the default status suggestions.

steps.heading: Customize your emoji and duration
steps.body: Adjust the status emoji if desired, then set 'Clear After' to 30 minutes or 1 hour.

steps.heading: Pause notifications and save
steps.body: Check the pause notifications box if you don't want alerts during lunch, then click Save.

faq.question: Will my status automatically revert after lunch?
faq.answer: Yes, once the expiration timer runs out, Slack automatically clears the status and restores your normal availability.

faq.question: Can I set a recurring lunch status every day?
faq.answer: Slack doesn't support fully automatic recurring statuses natively, so you'll need to set it manually or use a workflow automation.

faq.question: Does pausing notifications during lunch block all alerts?
faq.answer: Yes, it silences desktop and mobile push notifications until the pause period ends.

faq.question: Is there a keyboard shortcut for updating status?
faq.answer: Yes, Slack supports a quick shortcut to open the status update window without navigating through menus.
